note: This task requires live web/GitHub research. The values below are a snapshot taken 2026-06-20 to anchor grading; star counts and exact rankings change over time, so the supervisor should judge methodology, correct identification of real MCP servers, verification quality, and enumeration discipline rather than exact star numbers.
what_is_an_mcp_server: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server is a program that exposes tools/resources/prompts to an MCP client (e.g. Claude Desktop, IDEs) over the MCP protocol, typically via stdio or HTTP/SSE transport. The task is to enumerate real MCP SERVERS, not awesome-lists, MCP clients/SDKs alone, or unrelated projects.

real_server_anchors_snapshot.what: framework for building MCP servers (also ships servers); verify whether the executor counts it as a server or a framework
the official reference servers collection (filesystem, fetch, git, etc.) - a primary anchor the executor should find

distractors_to_exclude.why: an awesome-LIST of servers, not a server itself - must not be counted as a server
a debugging/inspector tool/client, not a server
a client-side library for using MCP, not a server

expected_fields_per_server: whether it is official/first-party or community
scoring_notes: Reward >=12 genuine MCP servers correctly identified and verified across the listed fields, with awesome-lists/clients/inspectors correctly EXCLUDED (or at least flagged as not-a-server). Reward including the official modelcontextprotocol/servers reference collection. Penalize counting awesome-lists or clients as servers, fabricated repos/links, or a list with no verification of what each server actually does. Exact star counts need not match the snapshot.
dynamic_content_note: GitHub stars and the MCP ecosystem change continuously. The snapshot anchors are illustrative; judge live findings on correctness, verification, and enumeration discipline, not on matching the snapshot exactly.
